mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 1.16% is substantially below the inflation rate implied by CPIAUCSL (247.592), creating negative real rates. This accommodative stance sustains equity valuations and reduces demand for safe-haven Treasuries, causing 10-year yields to rise over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into risk assets.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline by more than 10 basis points over the 20-day window despite negative real rates persisting, or FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5% without a corresponding yield increase.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.1% combined with payroll employment at 147,241 thousand indicates a tight labor market. Sustained tight labor conditions signal persistent wage pressure and inflation expectations, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, which compresses Treasury valuations and raises 10-year yields.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.3% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: 10-year yields fall by more than 15 basis points despite UNRATE staying below 4.3% and PAYEMS growth remaining positive, or wage inflation expectations (implied from market pricing) decline without a corresponding yield decline.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS at 492,717 million) and housing starts (HOUST at 1,290 thousand) are both elevated, indicating robust aggregate demand. Strong demand pressures inflation expectations and signals the economy is operating above potential, prompting expectations of higher terminal rates and causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5% or HOUST exceeds 1,250 thousand units
failure_condition: 10-year yields decline by more than 12 basis points over 20 days despite both RSAFS and HOUST remaining elevated, or subsequent CPI prints show disinflation without a corresponding yield decline.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index at 106.3969 (2017=100) reflects supply-side capacity utilization. If INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month over the next 20 trading days, it signals demand destruction or supply shock, reducing inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as investors seek safety.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year yields rise by more than 8 basis points following a month-over-month INDPRO decline exceeding 0.5%, or subsequent payroll data shows employment growth acceleration without yield decline.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 17,169.733 billion chained 2017 dollars) represents the broadest measure of economic activity. Sustained GDP growth above 2.5% annualized signals an economy operating above trend, increasing inflation risk and prompting higher terminal rate expectations, which raises 10-year Treasury yields.
failure_condition: 10-year yields decline by more than 20 basis points over a 60-day window despite GDP growth remaining above 2.5% annualized, or subsequent GDP revisions show growth below 2.0% without prior yield decline.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between inflation (CPIAUCSL trajectory) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS at 1.16%) is widening, indicating real rates are becoming increasingly negative. If this spread widens beyond 2.5 percentage points, it signals policy is too loose, forcing eventual tightening expectations that raise 10-year yields as markets price in future rate increases.
candidate_trigger: real_rate_spread widens to exceed negative 2.5 percentage points
regime_hint: monetary policy accommodation with inflation above target
failure_condition: 10-year yields fall by more than 10 basis points despite real rate spread exceeding negative 2.5 percentage points, or Federal Reserve signals explicit rate cuts without yield decline.

mechanism_narrative: If unemployment rate (UNRATE at 4.1%) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the next 20 trading days, it signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers a flight-to-safety bid in Treasuries, causing 10-year yields to fall as investors reduce equity exposure and demand safe assets.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year yields rise by more than 8 basis points following a month-over-month UNRATE increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points, or subsequent payroll data shows employment growth acceleration without yield rise.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 106.3969) combined with payroll employment (PAYEMS at 147,241 thousand) reveals supply-side constraints. If INDPRO growth decelerates while PAYEMS remains elevated, it signals stagflationary pressures where supply cannot meet demand, raising inflation expectations and causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as investors demand compensation for inflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth falls below 0.2% while PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.15%
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with supply constraints and labor tightness
failure_condition: 10-year yields decline by more than 15 basis points despite INDPRO deceleration and PAYEMS strength persisting, or subsequent CPI data shows disinflation without a corresponding yield decline.
